rulings.Case Title: JOY ANDERSON VS. CITIAPARTMENTS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Establishing Admissions, Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories, And For An Award Of Sanctions Against Deft'S
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOY ANDERSON, NICOLAS HARR Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Establishing Admissions, Compelling Responses To Form Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line Interrogatories, And For An Award Of Sanctions Against Deft'S Pro Tem Judge Barri Bonapart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.=302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Redacted) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel The Personal Contact Information Of The 2008 H-20 Examination Raters, Percipient Witnesses, Req. In The Special Interrogatories, Set One, Of Raycardo Aviles; Statement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs JOHN DANNER, DONALD DURKEE, RICHARD COTTON and RAYCARDO AVILES' (Redacted) Motion To Compel The Personal Contact Information Of The 2008 H-20 Examination Raters, Percipient Witnesses, Request In The Special Interrogatories, Set One, Of Raycardo Aviles. Denied. Defendants have provided the business contact information of the raters. Plaintiffs have not shown why this is not sufficient and why personal contact information should be required to be produced. The request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ST. FRANCIS - ONE CRESCENT WAY CONDOMINIUM VS. TOP VISION DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES E. ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Granted. Given the evidence concerning the likelihood of liability, the likely allocation of any liability, and the absence of collusion, the contesting party has not shown that the settlement is outside of the ballpark under the Tech-Bilt factors. rulings.Case Title: PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RICHARD GREENLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended X-Cpomplaint For Express Indemnity; Implied Indemnity; Dclaration Relief; Breach Of Contract; And Breach Of Implied Covenant Of Good Faith And Fair Dealing;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 9, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHANNES HUWYLER's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross Complaint For Express Indemnity; Implied Indemnity; Declaration Relief; Breach Of Contract; And Breach Of Implied Covenant Of Good Faith And Fair Dealing. Granted. Within 10 days, Cross-Complainant Huwyler shall file the First Amended Cross-Complaint. The only opposition to the motion was filed by Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company, and it does not provide a basis to deny the motion. No trial date has been set. Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company has not demonstrated prejudice to warrant denial of the motion, or any basis to permit the amendment with the imposition of conditions. rulings.Case Title: PAUL BRENNER et al VS. SUTTER HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court overrules the demurer to the first cause of action. Plaintiff alleges decedent was neglected for long period of times and that conduct can constitute reckless conduct to support the claim under Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23. The Court overrules the demurrer to the third cause of action because plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support the claim. The Court sustains without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. Plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts that he was present at the scene of the injury producing event at the time it occurred. See Thing v. La Chusa (1989) 48 Cal.3d 644, 647. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: O'HARA, BRIAN BY AND THROUGH HIS SUCCESSOR-IN- et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action, and overruled as to the first cause of action. On a demurrer, the facts pled in the complaint are assumed to be true. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts with the requisite particularity to allege professional negligence against Defendant City and County San Francisco ("Defendant"). For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege elder or dependent abuse. The allegations of reckless, oppression, fraud and malice are conclusory. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend the second cause of action, if he can do so in good faith, to allege that Defendant's neglect of the Decedent was reckless or done with oppression, fraud or malice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Quash In Whole Or In Part Deft Pg&E'S Overbroad Records Subpopena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, July 23, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Quash In Whole Or In Part Deft Pg&E'S Overbroad Records Subpopena Pro Tem Judge Barri Bonapart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/mjm
